...
Data Scientist AI ML Engineer at [PepsiCo](https://www.linkedin.com/company/pepsico)
...
I’m a Data Scientist / AI–ML Engineer with hands-on experience building machine learning, NLP, and LLM-driven solutions that make a real impact in production. My work spans the full end-to-end pipeline—cleaning and preparing data, designing features, training and tuning models, deploying them into cloud environments, and maintaining them through strong MLOps practices. I’ve worked on a variety of applied ML use cases, including forecasting, classification, recommendation systems, and text-based applications such as sentiment analysis, summarization, and embedding-powered retrieval. I enjoy working with modern AI frameworks like PyTorch, transformer models, and RAG workflows, especially when turning unstructured text into practical insights and automation. On the engineering side, I regularly use tools such as Databricks, PySpark, Airflow, MLflow, AWS, and Docker to build scalable pipelines and reproducible experiment setups. I like designing ML system architectures, integrating models into APIs, and ensuring reliability through monitoring, versioning, and automated retraining. I value clarity, collaboration, and building solutions that are not only technically sound but genuinely useful for the business. I’m continually exploring new developments in LLMs, retrieval systems, and MLOps—and I enjoy applying these ideas to create AI systems that are efficient, explainable, and built for real-world use.
...
### Data Scientist AI ML Engineer at [PepsiCo](https://www.linkedin.com/company/pepsico) (Current)
...
In my current role as a Data Scientist / AI–ML Engineer at Pepsico, I work across the full lifecycle of machine learning systems—from shaping raw data into usable features to deploying, monitoring, and improving production models. I build reliable end-to-end pipelines using Python, PyTorch, scikit-learn, MLflow, and Airflow, and collaborate closely with engineering and data teams to create scalable ML architectures on AWS and Databricks. My work spans forecasting, classification, recommendation systems, and NLP/LLM applications. I’ve developed text-based solutions such as sentiment analysis, document summarization, and embedding-driven retrieval using transformer models and RAG pipelines. I also focus heavily on MLOps, including experiment tracking, CI/CD for ML workflows, model versioning, drift detection, and automated retraining. Along with delivering production-ready models, I build monitoring and explainability frameworks using SHAP and custom dashboards to ensure fairness, accuracy, and stability. I enjoy prototyping new AI capabilities—especially in the generative and LLM space—and documenting system designs so the solutions we build are reproducible, maintainable, and ready for long-term adoption.

Over the years, the spotlight has shifted from Data Science to GenAI and now Agentic-AI. But people barely discuss Optimization, or more broadly Operations Research (OR), despite how powerful these tools are and the massive value they can help businesses unlock!
...
Data Science is mainly about learning from historical data and finding patterns. Hence, can help describe the past or forecast the future. GenAI generates content (code, images, text, etc.) at scale from users’ prompts. Agents shift from creation to action. They carry out complex tasks autonomously. Agentic-AI orchestrates end-to-end business processes through networks of agents that collaborate autonomously. All these tools rely heavily on “good” quality training data that can sometimes be massive depending on the scope, scale, and complexity of the problem. However, none of them inherently determine the best course of action. That’s where Optimization stands out.
...
Optimization is about making decisions, and good decisions lead to value generation. It is fundamentally prescriptive not predictive. Optimization models don’t learn from historical data. They evaluate choices, constraints, and trade-offs to recommend the best (optimal) decision that can be made. For example: Which supplier should be selected? Where should a new facility be opened? How many resources should be assigned to a given shift? At which price should a product (or service) be sold?
...
A prediction without a decision is just an insight. Automation without direction is just an activity. It can drive efficiency that can be massive initially but diminishes over time when a new norm is set. But a good optimization model provides a way to turn uncertainty and complexity into clear actionable decisions. The quality of the solution though depends on how well the model represents the real problem. It is an important skill that is undervalued especially in today’s AI-centric landscape.
...
Yes, an ML model or even an Agent can predict potential churn or changes in demand, but they won’t tell you the best retention strategy or the optimal inventory plan.

highlights: Overview Job Title: Associate Manager of Data Management and Operations Employer: PepsiCo, Inc. Location: 7701 Legacy Drive, Plano, Texas 75024 Responsibilities Duties: Manage a team of data engineers, including mentoring, process management, and vetting designs. Oversee work related to structuring and storing data into unified taxonomies and linking them with standard identifiers. Actively contribute into code development in projects and services. Manage and scale data pipelines from internal and external data sources to support new product launches and drive data quality across data products. Build and own the automation and monitoring frameworks that captures metrics and operational KPIs for data pipeline quality and performance. Implement best practices around data engineering, data science, BI, and other systems integration, security, performance and data management. Collaborate with internal clients (data science and product teams) to drive solutioning and POC discussions. Evolve the architectural capabilities and maturity of the data platform by engaging with enterprise architects and strategic internal and external partners. Define and manage SLA's for data products and processes running in production. Prototype new approaches and build solutions at scale. Create documentation for learnings and knowledge transfer. Create and audit reusable packages or libraries. *Telecommuting permitted 100%: work may be performed in any location in the U.S. #LI-DNI Qualifications Job Requirements: Bachelor's degree (US or Foreign Equivalent) in Computer Science, Electronic Engineering, or other technical fields and six (6) years of experience in job offered or related role. Must have four (4) years' experience with: Data Lake Infrastructure, Data Warehousing, and Data Analytics tool; Development experience in programming languages: Python, PySpark, and SQL; and SQL optimization and performance tuning. Must have two (2) years' experience with: Cloud data engineering experience in Azure, with fluency in Azure cloud services; Data modeling, data warehousing, and building high-volume ETL/ELT pipeline; at least one MPP database technology such as Redshift, Synapse or SnowFlake; and Azure Data Factory, Azure Databricks and Azure Machine learning tools.
